parameters.query: select o.id, o.order_number, cp.phone, ds.start_time, ds.end_time from orders o join customer_profiles cp on cp.id = o.customer_id left join delivery_slots ds on ds.id = o.delivery_slot_id where o.status = 'confirmed' and o.scheduled_date = current_date;

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ messaging_product: 'whatsapp', to: $json.phone, type: 'text', text: { body: `Good morning! 🌅 Your Aeden Fresh order ${$json.order_number} is being prepared and will arrive between ${$json.start_time} and ${$json.end_time}.` } }) }}

parameters.query: select phone, customer_id from ( select ws.phone, ws.customer_id, ws.last_message_at as activity_at from whatsapp_sessions ws where ws.state in ('cart','customising') union all select cp.phone, ae.customer_id, max(ae.created_at) as activity_at from analytics_events ae join customer_profiles cp on cp.id = ae.customer_id where ae.event_name = 'cart_activity' and not exists ( select 1 from analytics_events op where op.customer_id = ae.customer_id and op.event_name = 'order_placed' and op.created_at > ae.created_at ) group by cp.phone, ae.customer_id ) candidates where activity_at < now() - interval '2 hours' and activity_at > now() - interval '48 hours' and customer_id is not null and not exists ( select 1 from notifications_log nl where nl.customer_id = candidates.customer_id and nl.type = 'abandoned_cart' and nl.sent_at > now() - interval '24 hours' );

nodes.name: Find Abandoned Sessions
nodes.notes: Dedup built into the query: max one abandoned-cart nudge per customer per 24h.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ messaging_product: 'whatsapp', to: $json.phone, type: 'text', text: { body: 'Your salad is still waiting to be finished 🥗 — reply MENU to pick up where you left off.' } }) }}

nodes.name: Payment Failed Webhook
nodes.notes: Called by the platform's Razorpay webhook handler (M3) on payment.failed. Body: { order_id, customer_id, phone, retry_link, attempt }.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ messaging_product: 'whatsapp', to: $json.body.phone, type: 'text', text: { body: `Your payment didn't go through 😔 — tap to retry: ${$json.body.retry_link}` } }) }}

nodes.name: Wait 8h
nodes.notes: 3 attempts over 24h: send → wait 8h → check payment status via platform API → if still unpaid and attempt < 3, resend; else flag order for manual follow-up (notifications_log type='failed_payment', status='failed').

parameters.query: with gaps as (select sc.id, sc.name, sc.customer_id, cp.phone, sc.last_ordered_at, greatest(coalesce(extract(epoch from (now() - sc.last_ordered_at)) / 86400 / nullif(sc.order_count, 0), 7), 3) as avg_gap_days from saved_combos sc join customer_profiles cp on cp.id = sc.customer_id where sc.order_count >= 2 and cp.whatsapp_opted_in) select * from gaps where last_ordered_at < now() - (avg_gap_days || ' days')::interval and not exists (select 1 from notifications_log nl where nl.customer_id = gaps.customer_id and nl.type = 'reorder_prompt' and nl.sent_at > now() - interval '7 days');

nodes.name: Find Reorder Candidates
nodes.notes: avg reorder gap ≈ days since first order / order_count, floor 3 days. Max one prompt per customer per week.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ messaging_product: 'whatsapp', to: $json.phone, type: 'interactive', interactive: { type: 'button', body: { text: `Time for your usual ${$json.name}? 🥗` }, action: { buttons: [ { type: 'reply', reply: { id: 'reorder_yes_' + $json.id, title: 'Order it! 🛒' } }, { type: 'reply', reply: { id: 'reorder_no', title: 'Not today' } } ] } } }) }}

nodes.name: Daily 9 AM Campaign Check
nodes.notes: Birthday/festival campaigns: date-matched. Festival dates maintained in a Code node (Onam, Vishu, Christmas, Diwali…); birthdays need a dob column (future schema addition) or a campaign list. Sends approved WhatsApp TEMPLATE messages (marketing category) with an offer code — template must be pre-approved in Meta Business Manager.

nodes.name: Festival Date Match
nodes.notes: Empty output = no campaign today, chain stops. On match: query opted-in customers → send template broadcast in batches (Meta rate limits) → log notifications_log type='birthday'/'festival'.
